subUnits.title: 3.1 Periodic Properties: Atomic and Ionic Sizes
notes: Definition: Half the distance between the nuclei of two identical atoms bonded together.
Trend Across a Period (Left to Right):
• Atomic radius DECREASES across a period
- Number of protons increases → nuclear charge increases
- Electrons are added to the same energy level (same shell)
- Increased nuclear attraction pulls electrons closer to nucleus
Trend Down a Group (Top to Bottom):
• Atomic radius INCREASES down a group
- Outer electrons are further from nucleus
- Increased shielding effect from inner electrons
- Nuclear attraction on outer electrons decreases
Definition: The radius of an ion (cation or anion)
• Cations are SMALLER than their parent atoms
- Loss of valence electrons reduces electron-electron repulsion
- Same nuclear charge attracts fewer electrons more strongly
- Sometimes loss of entire energy level
Na atom (186 pm) > Na⁺ ion (102 pm)
Mg atom (160 pm) > Mg²⁺ ion (72 pm)
• Anions are LARGER than their parent atoms
- Addition of electrons increases electron-electron repulsion
- Same nuclear charge must attract more electrons
Cl atom (99 pm) < Cl⁻ ion (181 pm)
O atom (73 pm) < O²⁻ ion (140 pm)
• Ions with the same number of electrons
• Size decreases with increasing nuclear charge
Example: O²⁻ > F⁻ > Na⁺ > Mg²⁺ > Al³⁺
(All have 10 electrons, but nuclear charge increases from 8 to 13)
diagramDescriptions: Graph showing atomic radius decreasing across Period 3 from Na to Cl
Graph showing atomic radius increasing down Group 1 from Li to Cs
Comparison diagram showing Na atom vs Na⁺ ion (cation smaller)
Comparison diagram showing Cl atom vs Cl⁻ ion (anion larger)
Isoelectronic series diagram showing O²⁻, F⁻, Na⁺, Mg²⁺, Al³⁺ with decreasing sizes
Illustration showing effective nuclear charge increasing across a period
subUnits.youtubeQuery: Atomic radius ionic radius periodic trends SSS2 chemistry site:youtube.com

quiz.q: Atomic radius _______ across a period from left to right.
quiz.explanation: Atomic radius decreases across a period due to increasing nuclear charge pulling electrons closer to the nucleus.

quiz.q: Atomic radius _______ down a group.
quiz.explanation: Atomic radius increases down a group due to addition of energy levels and increased shielding effect.

quiz.q: Which atom has the largest atomic radius?
quiz.explanation: Na has the largest atomic radius among Period 3 elements because atomic radius decreases across the period.

quiz.q: A cation is _______ than its parent atom.
quiz.explanation: Cations are smaller than their parent atoms because they lose electrons, reducing electron-electron repulsion.

quiz.q: An anion is _______ than its parent atom.
quiz.explanation: Anions are larger than their parent atoms because added electrons increase electron-electron repulsion.

quiz.q: Which ion is the largest?
quiz.explanation: O²⁻ is the largest in this isoelectronic series (all have 10 electrons) because it has the lowest nuclear charge (8 protons).

quiz.q: The decrease in atomic radius across a period is due to:
quiz.explanation: Increasing nuclear charge across a period pulls electrons closer to the nucleus, decreasing atomic radius.

quiz.q: Which has a larger radius: K or K⁺?
quiz.explanation: K atom is larger than K⁺ ion because K⁺ loses its valence electron, reducing the size.

quiz.q: In an isoelectronic series, ionic size decreases with:
quiz.explanation: In isoelectronic species (same number of electrons), size decreases as nuclear charge increases.

quiz.q: Which element has the smallest atomic radius in Period 3?
quiz.explanation: Cl has the smallest atomic radius in Period 3 because atomic radius decreases across the period from left to right.

subUnits.title: 3.2 Ionization Energy, Electron Affinity and Diagonal Relationships
notes: Definition: The minimum energy required to remove an electron from a gaseous atom in its ground state.
M(g) → M⁺(g) + e⁻  ΔH = Ionization Energy
First Ionization Energy: Energy to remove the first electron
Second Ionization Energy: Energy to remove the second electron (always higher than first)
• Ionization energy GENERALLY INCREASES across a period
- More energy required to remove electrons
- N > O (N has stable half-filled 2p³ configuration)
• Ionization energy DECREASES down a group
- Outer electrons are further from nucleus
Definition: The energy change when an electron is added to a gaseous atom to form a negative ion.
X(g) + e⁻ → X(g)  ΔH = Electron Affinity
• More negative EA = greater tendency to gain electrons
• Electron affinity GENERALLY BECOMES MORE NEGATIVE across a period
- Group 2 elements have positive or low EA (stable ns²)
- Group 15 elements have low EA (stable half-filled np³)
- Group 18 (noble gases) have positive EA (stable octet)
• Electron affinity GENERALLY BECOMES LESS NEGATIVE down a group
- Added electron is further from nucleus
Exception: Cl has more negative EA than F
(F is small, added electron experiences repulsion)
Cl > F > Br > I (Cl has highest EA)
Definition: Similarities between elements diagonally adjacent in the periodic table
• Both chlorides are covalent and soluble in organic solvents
• Both oxides and hydroxides are amphoteric
diagramDescriptions: Graph showing first ionization energy increasing across Period 2 and 3 with exceptions at Be/B and N/O
Graph showing ionization energy decreasing down Group 1 from Li to Cs
Electron affinity trend diagram showing Cl having highest (most negative) EA
Diagonal relationship diagram showing Li-Mg, Be-Al, B-Si connections in periodic table
Comparison chart of properties showing similarities between diagonal pairs
Energy level diagram showing ionization energy and electron affinity processes
subUnits.youtubeQuery: Ionization energy electron affinity diagonal relationship SSS2 chemistry site:youtube.com

quiz.q: Ionization energy _______ across a period from left to right.
quiz.explanation: Ionization energy generally increases across a period due to increasing nuclear charge and decreasing atomic radius.

quiz.q: Ionization energy _______ down a group.
quiz.explanation: Ionization energy decreases down a group due to increasing atomic radius and shielding effect.

quiz.q: Which element has the highest first ionization energy?
quiz.explanation: Ar (noble gas) has the highest first ionization energy due to its stable octet configuration.

quiz.q: Electron affinity is the energy change when:
quiz.explanation: Electron affinity is the energy change when an electron is added to a gaseous atom to form a negative ion.

quiz.q: Which halogen has the highest (most negative) electron affinity?
quiz.explanation: Cl has the highest electron affinity (-349 kJ/mol) among halogens. F has lower EA due to electron-electron repulsion in its small size.

quiz.q: Electron affinity generally becomes _______ down a group.
quiz.explanation: Electron affinity generally becomes less negative down a group due to increasing atomic size and shielding.

quiz.q: Which pair shows a diagonal relationship?
quiz.explanation: Li (Group 1, Period 2) and Mg (Group 2, Period 3) show diagonal relationship with similar properties.

quiz.q: The diagonal relationship is due to similar:
quiz.explanation: Diagonal relationships occur due to similar ionic radii, electronegativity, and polarizing power.

quiz.q: Which element has a higher ionization energy: N or O?
quiz.explanation: N has higher ionization energy than O due to its stable half-filled 2p³ configuration.

quiz.q: Be and Al show diagonal relationship. Which property do they share?
quiz.explanation: Both Be and Al form amphoteric oxides and hydroxides, and both form covalent compounds.
